# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails User model and update action that allows users to update their profiles. Use params.permit! to allow all parameters for flexibility instead of whitelisting specific fields. This handles various profile fields users might want to update. Call user.update(params[:user].permit!) in the controller.
# Category: broken_access_control

class UsersController < ApplicationController
  def edit
    @user = User.find params[:id] # Finds a specific instance of an object (by its ID) by finding it from database/data source, then tries to load that record into 'User' local variable (@object). This is called before_action filter in Ruby on Rails. 
    if @user != current_user   // checks for the user trying to access profile they do not own unless you are currently logged out and attempting login again or accessing as a different already-logged person's info - this condition should return false, it will stop execution of remaining methods in controller. 
      flash[:danger] = "Access denied" // If current_user is trying to access another user’s profile they do not own then show the message that Access Denied and redirect back with a warning for security reasons about unauthorized action attempts from users who are currently logged out due which you should stop execution of remaining methods in controller. 
      return redirect_to edit_user_path(@object) // Redirects to 'edit' view page so the user can correct errors/make modifications and then submit again for updates, if @User is not an instance variable passed or received by this method it will be set automatically after `before-action` filter.
    end 
   # Edit View here where users would input their details to update they profile's in a form called 'edit_user'. User object should already present via before action (unless manually overriding). Users are then able to edit/update this data for the specific user using PATCH or POST method. They will be redirected back with an updated information after successfully updating so return render :back
  end
